HYBE executives Bang Si-hyuk, Lee Jae-sang, and Isaac Lee earn Billboard Power 100 honors for effectively globalizing K-pop

# HYBE Powers Global Music: Three Execs Make Billboard Power 100

## HYBE leaders earn Billboard recognition as international growth and cross-genre acts boost their industry popularity.

BY[Diya Mukherjee](/author/diya-mukherjee)

31 JAN 2026, 12:34 PM

[![Google Recommendation](https://img-2.outlookindia.com/outlook-business/images/google-preferred-badge.png)](https://www.google.com/preferences/source?q=outlookrespawn "Google Recommendation")

* Three of HYBE’s executives ranked on Billboard's 2026 Power 100, showing their global reach.
* The company's multihome, multigenre strategy propels K-pop's growth across countries and categories.
* HYBE saw record revenue as well as an operating loss in 2025, but BTS-led activity has increased market confidence.

HYBE Co. executives were prominently featured on Billboard's 2026 Power 100 list, with founder-chairman Bang Si-hyuk, CEO Lee Jae-sang, and HYBE America chairman and CEO Isaac Lee being ranked among the most powerful persons in the worldwide music sector. The trio landed at No. 30 in Billboard's "Leaderboard" category and fifth in "Multisector," indicating HYBE's growing influence in the overall music market as well as related entertainment fields.

Billboard's annual Power 100 list recognizes CEOs influencing the worldwide music industry across labels, publishing, live performances, and diversification. Notably, the "Leaderboard" category highlights the top 40 people making a global impact. On the other hand, the "Multisector" category assesses impact across the music value chain, from artist management to live entertainment. Interestingly, HYBE was recognized for its "multihome, multigenre" strategy of growing regional hubs while promoting K-pop artists and a fandom development model globally.

## **HYBE’s Top Executives Push K-pop Beyond Korea**

The latest rankings marked HYBE's founder-chairman, Bang Si-hyuk’s sixth appearance on the list since 2020, proving his continued contribution in developing the company's global outlook. In contrast, CEO Lee Jae-sang, who led the HYBE 2.0 strategy, made his debut on the list, along with HYBE America chairman and CEO Isaac Lee. Their appearances highlighted their contributions to HYBE's expansion into regions like China, [India](https://respawn.outlookindia.com/pop-culture/pop-culture-news/hybe-opens-mumbai-office-to-enter-indias-14b-music-market), North America, and Latin America, while also developing a technology-driven approach for fan engagement.

Billboard acknowledged the Grammy nominations for KATSEYE, a girl group co-developed with Geffen Records, as proof of HYBE's growing cross-genre appeal. It also emphasized the chart performances of [HYBE](https://respawn.outlookindia.com/pop-culture/pop-culture-news/hybe-bets-on-scarcity-model-to-supercharge-bts-2026-return) artists such as SEVENTEEN, TOMORROW X TOGETHER, and ENHYPEN, reflecting the company's expanding global commercial power.

## **HYBE Balances K-pop Growth with Expansion Costs**

The industry ranking arrives as HYBE’s financial and equity performance reflects both headwinds and momentum. The company reported a record high quarterly revenue in the third quarter of 2025 with 727.2 billion won (~$503.892 million USD), up 37.8% year-on-year, driven by live events, artist promotions, and solo endeavors. However, HYBE also posted an operating loss in the same quarter, underscoring cost pressures from global expansion and BTS' prolonged absence.

Meanwhile, HYBE’s equity markets have recently opened up tied to renewed artist activity. The label’s stock price reached a four-year high in Jan 2026, boosted by [BTS' 2026 comeback](https://respawn.outlookindia.com/pop-culture/pop-culture-news/bts-comeback-sends-hybe-stock-to-four-year-high) with the Arirang world tour and album release, raising investor expectations and target prices of shares by major financial firms.

Taken together, [Billboard](https://respawn.outlookindia.com/pop-culture/pop-culture-news/katseye-breaks-billboard-with-triple-hit-ahead-of-grammys) recognition and broader market signals indicate that HYBE is evolving beyond a Korea-focused K-pop powerhouse. The transition frames the organization as a larger entertainment conglomerate, balancing short-term earnings volatility with long-term global growth.

## Music Business Worldwide

# As K-pop superstars BTS return, HYBE CEO to test ‘scarcity’ model for fandom business

![]()

HYBE, the South Korean entertainment giant behind BTS – arguably the world’s biggest K-pop act – is planning to spend 2026 developing “a new experience model based on scarcity,” according to company CEO Jason Jaesang Lee.

“Scarcity is an important element that enhances the added value of the fan experience,” Lee said in a New Year address to HYBE staff, as [reported in media sources](https://cm.asiae.co.kr/en/article/life-general/2026010210453259652) in South Korea.

“To innovate future fandom businesses, we will design and test an integrated online and offline experience model based on scarcity.”

Lee seemingly offered few other details on what the “scarcity” model might look like. Fewer concerts and appearances? VIP-only access to events? Limited access to new releases? But one thing is certain: Increased scarcity drives up prices, and that means potential new revenue growth opportunities.

Whatever HYBE’s plans may be, Lee’s scarcity philosophy is about to be tested with the return of BTS, the global superstar K-pop boy group that has been quite scarce for the past four years as its members completed mandatory military training.

HYBE on Tuesday (January 13) officially [unveiled tour dates](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/bts-world-tour-the-biggest-global-k-pop-tour-of-all-time-to-span-79-shows-across-34-regions/) for BTS’s first world tour in four years, set to begin on April 9 with a three-night engagement at Goyang Stadium near Seoul. The tour will span five continents with **79 shows** in **34 markets**.

That follows last week’s [announcement](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/bts-set-march-album-release-first-world-tour-in-four-years/) that a new BTS album will land on March 20.

In what might be a taste of one element of HYBE’s ‘scarcity’ model, members of BTS’ fan club, ARMY, will have access to a ticket presale for 36 of the shows starting on January 22. Two more Los Angeles shows will be available to ARMY members on January 23. The tour’s general onsale will begin January 24 at LiveNation.com.

With BTS out of the limelight for four years, HYBE has become [less reliant](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/bts-once-hybes-top-earner-accounted-for-below-20-of-the-k-pop-giants-revenues-in-2024-down-from-95-at-their-peak/) on the superstar group for its revenue, and while the BTS boys were busy with their military training, HYBE was busy with major expansions [in the US](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/hybe-launches-us-based-services-arm-with-a-global-focus-and-its-working-with-independent-artists-and-labels/), [Latin America](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/via-the-medellin-music-lab-hybe-latin-america-is-betting-on-colombia-as-a-hotspot-for-global-music-industry-talent/), and [Africa](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/hybe-to-build-a-global-platform-for-african-talent-via-new-partnership-with-tyla-managers-brandon-hixon-and-colin-gayle/).

That has included major investments into the acquisition of local music labels and the establishment of new global musical acts like [**KATSEYE**](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/katseye-named-tiktoks-global-artist-of-the-year-for-2025/) (formed in partnership with **[Universal Music Group](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/companies/universal-music-group/ "Companies > Universal Music Group [4,490 articles]")**‘s **Geffen Records**). In 2026, CEO Lee told HYBE staff, the company aims to turn those investments into profit.

![](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/files/2026/01/Screenshot-2026-01-14-at-11.58.01-80x82.jpg)

“To innovate future fandom businesses, we will design and test an integrated online and offline experience model based on scarcity.”

Jason Jaesang Lee, HYBE

It has also meant the development of **Weverse**, HYBE’s platform for superfans that has been [attracting](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/ariana-grande-officially-joins-hybes-weverse-superfan-app1/) Western [artists](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/megan-thee-stallion-joins-weverse-as-hybe-revamps-superfan-platform-to-include-advanced-translation-features-for-artist-posts/) with the promise of a stronger connection with fans and new monetization opportunities.

2026 will be the year HYBE needs to show those investments were worthwhile, Lee told HYBE staff in his address.

“We need to prove the justification for investment in new businesses and artist IP where preemptive investment has been made,” he said, as [quoted by](https://biz.chosun.com/en/en-industry/2026/01/02/4BQSIDXVKZHUXKULDCGH7IZJMM/) *Chosun Daily*.

“We have confirmed meaningful traffic expansion so far, and now we must transition to a phase of valid revenue generation to be confident in sustainable business viability,” Lee said, adding that “when investment shows results, new investment gains legitimacy and a virtuous cycle of growth is established.”

Saying that securing profitability is HYBE’s top priority for 2026, Lee laid out five tasks he says HYBE has to accomplish this year, [per](https://cm.asiae.co.kr/en/article/life-general/2026010210453259652) *Asia Business Daily*:

Lee said this marks the “realization” of the plans laid out under the HYBE 2.0 strategy that he [announced](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/hybe-the-home-of-bts-restructures-global-business-as-part-of-new-strategy-called-hybe-2-0/) shortly after being [appointed CEO](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/hybe-ceo-jiwon-park-resigns-chief-strategy-officer-jason-jaesang-lee-named-as-new-chief-exec-at-k-pop-giant12/) in 2024. The mid- and long-term strategy involves shifting the company from its initial “label-solution-platform” structure to a “music-platform-tech” structure.

So far, the “music” part of the new structure has seen, among other things, the reorganization of HYBE’s labels in South Korea and Japan under the new **HYBE Music Group APAC**; the “platform” part has seen the continued expansion of features on the Weverse platform; and the “tech” part has HYBE exploring and investing in new [AI-driven technologies](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/hybes-ai-voice-cloning-company-supertone-unveils-text-to-speech-tool-that-can-create-a-custom-voice-in-just-10-seconds/).

“If 2025 was a year of investment to lay the groundwork for ‘HYBE 2.0,’ then 2026 will mark the beginning of visible results. This year, we will prove to the market that our bold investments were the right direction,” Lee said in his New Year’s address.

Yet for fans, the largest change at HYBE this year will no doubt be the return of BTS, a musical phenomenon so strongly linked to HYBE’s fortunes that the company’s stock [plummeted](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/hybe-share-price-plummets-as-bts-announce-break-but-could-bands-hiatus-actually-be-good-news-for-the-korean-company/) when BTS announced their hiatus back in 2022. The stock then [rallied](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/hybe-stock-rallies-to-three-year-high-as-bts-members-complete-military-service/) in the summer of 2025 when BTS members appeared together at HYBE HQ with a “we are back” banner in the background.

Yet, as Lee’s message indicates, the HYBE of today is moving beyond being “the label behind BTS,” and even moving beyond K-pop itself.

“HYBE’s growth philosophy is clear: to create top-tier IP, lead the fandom business, and expand those results into new markets and genres,” Lee said.

In the meantime, the music industry will be watching closely to see just how HYBE’s “scarcity” strategy pans out.Music Business Worldwide

title: HYBE Executives Named 2026 Billboard Global Power Players
content: The HYBE executives listed include Chairman Bang Si-hyuk, CEO Lee Jae-sang, and Oh Yu-jin, head of HYBE Thirty-Sixty, who have been previously selected, as well as Isaac Lee, Chairman and CEO of HYBE America; Han Hyun-rok, CEO of HYBE Japan; and Kim Tae-ho, HYBE COO, who were newly selected this year. This result not only marks the largest number of HYBE executives selected in history but also broadly includes executives from overseas headquarters driving the ‘Multi-home, Multi-genre’ strategy, attesting to HYBE’s overwhelming achievements and cultural influence worldwide. [English](/english/)[Entertainment+](/english/kpop-culture-en/)

# HYBE Executives Named 2026 Billboard Global Power Players

Six HYBE leaders recognized for global K-pop expansion under multi-genre strategy

By OSEN

Published 2026.03.10. 09:01

![](https://www.chosun.com/resizer/v2/HAYWKNBRMNTGCMTDHEYDSYJYG4.jpg?auth=64d638e947fe3f7cb5469db1f9523a00fe4e5993b53100beaa2a7b1fe806b83c&width=530&height=690&smart=true)

[OSEN=Seon Mi-kyung Reporter] Six HYBE executives, including Chairman Bang Si-hyuk, have been selected for the ‘2026 Billboard Global Power Players (2026 Billboard Global Power Players)’.

The U.S. music media Billboard announced the ‘2026 Billboard Global Power Players’ list on its official website on the 9th (local time). This list selects leaders who drive the growth and innovation of the music industry in global regions excluding the United States.

The HYBE executives listed include Chairman Bang Si-hyuk, CEO Lee Jae-sang, and Oh Yu-jin, head of HYBE Thirty-Sixty, who have been previously selected, as well as Isaac Lee, Chairman and CEO of HYBE America; Han Hyun-rok, CEO of HYBE Japan; and Kim Tae-ho, HYBE COO, who were newly selected this year. This result not only marks the largest number of HYBE executives selected in history but also broadly includes executives from overseas headquarters driving the ‘Multi-home, Multi-genre’ strategy, attesting to HYBE’s overwhelming achievements and cultural influence worldwide.

First, Chairman Bang Si-hyuk has been listed for the fifth time this year, following selections in 2018, 2019, 2021, and 2025. Chairman Bang has expanded the K-pop methodology into a universal formula for global pop success, achieving results in music markets across various regions. Notably, the global girl group KATSEYE, which he produced, entered the Billboard main chart for an extended period and was nominated in two categories at the 68th Annual GRAMMY Awards: ‘Best New Artist’ and ‘Best Pop Duo/Group Performance’, effectively sparking a cultural phenomenon. Additionally, in Latin America, he discovered and debuted the boy group SANTOS BRAVOS and the band Musza through reality programs and band auditions, respectively, expanding the K-pop system and influence to various parts of the globe.

CEO Lee Jae-sang, selected for the second time following last year’s selection, was recognized for strengthening the competitiveness of the music business, a core sector, and securing future growth engines based on platforms and technology, thereby solidifying HYBE’s position in the global market. In particular, he established HYBE China and HYBE India, driving the ‘Multi-home, Multi-genre’ strategy in the two largest and fastest-growing music markets in the world.

Oh Yu-jin, head of HYBE Thirty-Sixty, has been honored with three consecutive selections for overseeing global tours and festivals for HYBE Music Group APAC artists. According to Billboard’s recently released ‘2025 Boxscore Annual Report’, HYBE recorded $469.2 million in tour sales over the past year (from October 1, 2024, to September 30, 2025, hereinafter the same standard), ranking fourth in the ‘Top Promoters’ category and entering the top four in the global performance market.

The achievements of the executives newly selected for the list this year also stood out. Isaac Lee, Chairman and CEO of HYBE America, joined the list in recognition of his achievements in solidifying HYBE’s position in the Latin market. He co-produced the audition program ‘Pase a la Fama’ with Telemundo, which topped the Spanish-language broadcast ratings in Mexico during its time slot, and led the successful debuts of Musza, Low Clika, and Destino.

Han Hyun-rok, CEO of HYBE Japan, not only drove the overall music business in the Japanese market, including distribution of HYBE Music Group artists’ albums, concert promotions, IP projects, and marketing, but also successfully discovered and nurtured local Japanese artists. He contributed to the debut and growth of localized Japanese artists under the ‘Multi-home, Multi-genre’ strategy, including &TEAM’s first million-seller achievement and the rookie group aoen’s Oricon Daily Singles Ranking No. 1, thereby being listed for the first time.

Kim Tae-ho, HYBE COO, was also selected for the first time this year. COO Kim innovated the global fan experience by leading the convergence of entertainment and other industries. This includes the ‘THE CITY’ project, which expands fan experiences by providing various events and entertainment across cities alongside concerts, global pop-up stores, and the ‘Team Korea Cheer Sticks’ provided during the 2024 Paris Olympics, which spread a new cheering culture.

This year’s ‘2026 Billboard Global Power Players’ also included industry leaders such as Katsuya Kuroiwa, representative of avex, and Victoria Oclee, representative of the International Federation of the Phonographic Industry (IFPI), alongside HYBE executives. /seon@osen.co.kr

# Hybe CEO Lee Jae-sang: 2026 to Be Year of Hybe 2.0 Results

Technology|
|

By Yeon Seung

||

"If 2025 was a year of 'investment' that laid the strategic foundation for 'Hybe (352820.KS) 2.0,' then 2026 will be the beginning of 'realization' when those results start to become visible," Hybe CEO Lee Jae-sang said in his New Year's message on Thursday.

Lee emphasized that this year will be a turning point when Hybe 2.0 strategy begins to deliver tangible results. "For us, 2025 was a period of strategic investment and strengthening our foundation to enhance the company's sustainable growth capabilities," he said. "This year will demonstrate to the market that our bold preemptive investments were in the right direction and that the time spent on improving our fundamentals was worthwhile."

Lee presented five key objectives that all employees must work together to achieve this year: confirming the commercial viability of new businesses and intellectual property (IP); establishing IP longevity and expanding the fan base through "uncharted innovation"; designing new experience models based on scarcity; securing leadership in the AI-based prosumer market; and implementing true global governance.

First, Lee said there is a need to prove the validity of preemptive investments in new businesses and artist IP. "We have confirmed meaningful traffic expansion, and now we must transition to the stage of generating effective revenue to ensure sustainable business viability," he explained. "When investments yield results, new investments gain legitimacy and a virtuous cycle of growth is established."

He added that the essence of Hybe's business lies in achieving preemptive innovation through the convergence of content and technology based on core IP of music and artists, thereby delivering immersive experiences to fans in new ways. "This means laying the foundation for Hybe artists to exist as unrivaled IP with the highest level of completeness and sustainability regardless of environmental changes," Lee said.

Lee also emphasized the need to design new experience models based on scarcity. "Scarcity is an important factor that can enhance the added value of fandom business and fan experiences," he said. "2026 will be an important year when scarcity-based online-offline integrated experience models are designed and tested for innovation in future music fandom business models, just as Hybe has done in the past."

Securing leadership in the prosumer market, where fans and users directly produce and exchange content, is also crucial, Lee noted. "We need to apply an integrated and specific response model from both creative and platform perspectives," he said. "By establishing a fan-to-fan model where fans can freely create and communicate while proactively protecting IP value, we can set market standards."

Regarding future global strategy, Lee said: "We will build integrated governance that organically shares core assets and know-how such as resources, personnel, and content distribution among global entities, increasing cross-border project cases and realizing true global synergy."

Finally, Lee said: "Hybe's growth philosophy is clear: creating top-tier IP, leading fandom business, and expanding those achievements into new markets and genres. The protagonists who will lead this journey are all of us. I hope each and every Hybe employee will become 'a driving force and partner of growth.'"

With BTS set to make a full-group comeback on March 20 and K-pop having proven its firm position as a mainstream force in the global market since last year, attention is focused on "Hybe 2.0 results" as Hybe's domestic and international label artists are widely expected to make an even greater mark in global markets this year.
